ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'coffee beverage' class (FOODON_03301710); no specific class exists for blended iced espresso frappuccino. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers coffee-based beverages broadly; no specific code for blended iced espresso frappuccino exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for blended iced espresso frappuccino beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Grande serving (16 fl oz / 473 g) is the standard Starbucks size for this blended iced espresso beverage, providing approximately 220 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Starbucks, Frappuccino, Coffee, Bottled (FDC ID 2103697) and Starbucks Espresso Frappuccino Blended Beverage nutritional panel
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are derived from the input record which references USDA FoodData Central Starbucks beverage data. The per-100g values (energy 46.51 kcal, protein 1.27 g, fat 0.53 g, carbohydrate 9.3 g, sugar 8.88 g, sodium 24.31 mg, dietary fibre 0.0 g, calcium 42.28 mg, iron 0.04 mg, cholesterol 2.11 mg, saturated fat 0.32 g, trans fat 0.0 g) are consistent with published Starbucks Espresso Frappuccino Grande nutritional data (220 kcal / 473 g serving = ~46.5 kcal/100g). Values accepted as adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Starbucks blended coffee beverages. Potassium estimated at ~80 mg/100g based on USDA FoodData Central Starbucks coffee beverage data; monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat data not available in the referenced source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for sweetened coffee-milk beverage
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks Espresso Frappuccino. GI estimated at 53 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened milk-coffee beverages and commercial coffee drinks with added sugar. Per the University of Sydney GI classification scale, GI 53 falls within the low GI range (0–55). The dominant glycaemic driver is sucrose from the Frappuccino base syrup (8.88 g sugar/100g). Glycemic load per Grande serving (473 g) calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (53 × 44.0) / 100 ≈ 23.3. Carbohydrate per serving = 9.3 g/100g × 473 g / 100 = 44.0 g. Note: although GI is low (53), the glycemic load per serving is high (23.3) due to the large serving size and substantial total carbohydrate content, making this beverage clinically significant for blood glucose management.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.88 g sugar/100g exceeding the 5 g/100g SSB threshold for beverages; caffeine from espresso may cause acute blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this sugar-sweetened blended coffee beverage; the high sugar content (8.88 g/100g) independently raises cardiovascular and blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines, and the caffeine from the espresso shot may cause acute transient blood pressure elevation — choose unsweetened espresso or Americano instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.88 g sugar/100g and zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar from Frappuccino base syrup causes rapid postprandial glucose elevation; high glycemic load of 23.3 per Grande serving despite low GI of 53
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it is a sugar-sweetened beverage (8.88 g sugar/100g, 0 g fibre/100g) with a high glycemic load of 23.3 per Grande serving driven by 44 g total carbohydrate, causing significant postprandial glucose spikes — choose plain espresso or Americano without added syrup, or request a sugar-free syrup substitution.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sugar content (8.88 g/100g) from added Frappuccino base syrup with negligible saturated fat (0.32 g/100g); regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is independently associated with increased CVD risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this sugar-sweetened blended coffee beverage; while saturated fat is low (0.32 g/100g) and sodium is low (24.31 mg/100g), the high added sugar content (8.88 g/100g) and high glycemic load per serving are independently associated with increased triglycerides and CVD risk per AHA dietary guidelines — choose unsweetened coffee alternatives.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High refined sugar content (8.88 g/100g) with zero dietary fibre promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; the anti-inflammatory polyphenol benefit of espresso is negated by the sugar-sweetened blended format
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular consumption of this sugar-sweetened blended coffee beverage as the refined sugar content (8.88 g/100g) promotes inflammatory mediators and negates the anti-inflammatory chlorogenic acid polyphenols present in espresso — choose plain espresso without added syrup to access coffee's anti-inflammatory benefits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Espresso Frappuccino is a blended iced coffee beverage sold by Starbucks, combining Frappuccino roast coffee, a shot of Starbucks Espresso Roast, low-fat milk, Frappuccino base syrup, and ice, blended to a smooth, creamy, chilled consistency. It is served without whipped cream in its standard form. The beverage has a sweet, milky coffee flavour with a pronounced espresso bitterness balanced by the sweetness of the Frappuccino base syrup. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 46.5 kcal, 1.27 g protein, 0.53 g fat (0.32 g saturated), 9.3 g carbohydrate, and 8.88 g sugar, with negligible dietary fibre (0 g). Sodium is low at 24.31 mg/100g. A standard Grande serving (473 g / 16 fl oz) delivers approximately 220 kcal and 44 g carbohydrate, predominantly from added sugar. Clinically, this is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) due to its sugar content exceeding 5 g/100g for a beverage, making it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 53 (low GI per University of Sydney classification scale of 0–55), however the glycemic load per Grande serving is high at approximately 23.3 due to the large serving volume and 44 g total carbohydrate, making it clinically significant for blood glucose management. The beverage is vegetarian, gluten-free, and contains dairy (milk allergen). It is widely available at Starbucks outlets across Singapore and Southeast Asia and is categorised as an ultra-processed commercial beverage.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Starbucks Espresso Frappuccino Grande?
What is the sugar content of Espresso Frappuccino?
Starbucks blended coffee drink calories Singapore

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods data for Starbucks blended coffee beverages, which is an approved secondary source. The per-100g values are internally consistent with the published Grande serving size (473 g, ~220 kcal). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened milk-coffee beverages as no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists. Potassium estimated at ~80 mg/100g based on USDA FoodData Central Starbucks coffee beverage data. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact USDA FoodData Central entry for this specific Starbucks Espresso Frappuccino blended beverage (as opposed to the bottled RTD version) was not directly confirmed, and GI is an analogous estimate.
notes: 1. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Starbucks beverage data — this blended coffee beverage contains no plant fibre sources. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero by product formulation — Starbucks Frappuccino beverages are formulated without partially hydrogenated oils. 3. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero — this beverage contains no vitamin C sources. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific blended beverage not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific Starbucks blended beverage not available in USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. 6. Potassium estimated at 80 mg/100g from comparable USDA FoodData Central Starbucks coffee beverage entries; not directly confirmed for this specific product. 7. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate for sweetened milk-coffee beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Starbucks Espresso Frappuccino exists in peer-reviewed literature. Per the University of Sydney GI classification scale, GI 53 is low (0–55 = low). gi_category corrected from 'medium' to 'low' to align with the University of Sydney three-tier classification (low: 0–55, medium: 56–69, high: 70+). Despite the low GI, the glycemic load per Grande serving is high (23.3) due to the large serving volume (473 g) and 44 g total carbohydrate. 8. The input record listed 'country_of_origin' as Singapore; corrected to United States as Frappuccino is a Starbucks (US-origin) branded product, though widely sold in Singapore. 9. Ethnic tags 'Cake' and 'Sandwich' removed from cultural classification as they are not applicable to this beverage. 10. This is a Western commercial branded beverage; multilingual local-name fields (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not applicable as the product is sold under its English brand name globally.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full RESULT JSON. Identified need to verify aliases consistency (q8 self-check), confirm zero-macro justifications in notes (g4), document all -1 fields (g3), and ensure GI analogous estimate is fully documented.

Iteration 2: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'. Added comprehensive notes documenting all -1 fields, zero-value justifications, GI analogous estimate basis, potassium estimation note, and country-of-origin correction. Verified aliases_by_language.english strings appear verbatim in flat aliases array.

Iteration 3: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' to align with the University of Sydney GI classification scale (GI 53 falls in the low range of 0–55, not medium which is 56–69). Updated gi_source.source_match_notes and notes field to document this correction and clarify the distinction between low GI and high glycemic load per serving. Updated type2_diabetes key_factor to reference the high glycemic load despite low GI. Updated natural_language_description to accurately reflect the low GI classification while noting the high glycemic load.
